beak.experimental.focal module

beak.experimental.focal._apply_binary_dilation(array: ndarray, kernel: ndarray, target_value: Number) → ndarray[source]

Apply binary dilation to the input array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The input array to be dilated.

  • kernel (np.ndarray) – The kernel or footprint to be used for dilation.

  • target_value (Number) – The value to be dilated.

Returns:

The dilated array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.focal._apply_generic_filter(array: ndarray, filter_fn: Callable, kernel: ndarray, *args) → ndarray[source]

Apply a generic filter to the input array.

Parameters:

  • array – The input array to be filtered.

  • filter_fn – The filter function to be applied.

  • kernel – The kernel or footprint to be used for filtering.

  • *args – Additional arguments to be passed to the filter function.

Returns:

The filtered array.

beak.experimental.focal._basic_kernel(radius: int, shape: Literal['square', 'circle']) → ndarray[source]

Generate a basic kernel of a specified size and shape.

Parameters:

  • radius – The radius of the kernel.

  • shape – The shape of the kernel. Can be either “square” or “circle”.

Returns:

The generated kernel.

beak.experimental.focal._check_filter_size(radius: int)[source]

Check the filter size and raise exceptions if it does not meet the requirements.

Parameters:

radius – The size of the filter kernel.

Raises:

Exception – If the resulting filter radius is too small.

beak.experimental.focal._create_grid(size: int, radius) → tuple[ndarray, ndarray][source]

Create a grid of coordinates.

Parameters:

size – The size of the grid.

Returns:

A tuple with x and y coordinates of the grid.

beak.experimental.focal._create_local_buffer_binary_version(array: ndarray, radius: int, shape: Literal['square', 'circle'], target_value: Number) → ndarray[source]

Creates a buffer with a constant value around the selected value in a given array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The array to be buffered.

  • radius (int) – Number of cells (distance) to the targeted value to be changed.

  • shape (Literal["square", "circle"]) – The shape of the buffer.

  • target_value (Number) – The value to be buffered.

Returns:

The buffered array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.focal._create_local_buffer_generic_version(array: ndarray, radius: int, shape: Literal['square', 'circle'], target_value: int) → ndarray[source]

Creates a buffer with a constant value around the selected value in a given array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The array to be buffered.

  • radius (int) – Number of cells (distance) to the targeted value to be changed.

  • shape (Literal["square", "circle"]) – The shape of the buffer.

  • target_value (int) – The value to be buffered.

Returns:

The buffered array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.focal._get_kernel_size(radius: int) → tuple[int, int][source]

Calculate the kernel size and on the given radius.

Parameters:

radius – The radius of the filter kernel.

Returns:

A tuple containing the calculated size and radius of the kernel.

beak.experimental.focal._replace_value_in_kernel(window: ndarray, target_value: Number) → Number[source]
beak.experimental.focal.create_simple_buffer(array: ndarray, target_value: int = 1, radius: int = 1, const: Number = nan) → ndarray[source]

Creates a buffer with a constant value around the selected value in a given array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The array to be buffered.

  • target_value (Number) – The value to be buffered. Defaults to 1.

  • radius (int) – Number of cells (distance) to the targeted value to be changed. Defaults to 1. E.g. radius of 1 will change the 8 cells around the targeted value.

  • const (Number) – The constant value to be used. Defaults to np.nan.

Returns:

The buffered array.

Return type:

np.ndarray
